# jurisdiction-kit

> Zero-dependency TypeScript library providing professional body registries, data protection law details, and jurisdiction intelligence for 28 countries. Look up compliance rules, check cross-border data transfer legality, find regulated professions, and rank jurisdictions by confidence score.

## Install

```
npm install jurisdiction-kit
```

ESM only. Zero runtime dependencies. Works in Node 14+.

## Covered jurisdictions (28)

GB, US, FR, DE, ES, IT, NL, CA, AU, NZ, JP, KR, BR, MX, AR, IN, SG, AE, SA, ZA, CN, HK, IE, TR, ID, NG, KE, IL.

## Profession types

`legal` | `medical` | `notary` | `accounting` | `engineering` | `teaching` | `financial` | `veterinary` | `pharmacy` | `architecture` | `social-work`

---

## Full API Reference

### `getJurisdiction(code: string): Jurisdiction | undefined`

Returns the full jurisdiction object by ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. Case-insensitive. Returns `undefined` for unknown codes.

```typescript
import { getJurisdiction } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const gb = getJurisdiction('GB')
// gb.name === 'United Kingdom'
// gb.nameLocal === 'United Kingdom'
// gb.legalSystem === 'common-law'
// gb.languages === ['en']
// gb.currency === 'GBP'
// gb.eSignatureRecognised === true
// gb.mutualRecognition === ['AU', 'NZ', 'CA', 'SG', 'HK', 'IE', ...]
// gb.dataProtection.name === 'UK GDPR + DPA 2018'
// gb.dataProtection.breachNotificationHours === 72
// gb.dataProtection.rightToErasure === true
// gb.childProtection.minAgeDigitalConsent === 13

const sg = getJurisdiction('sg')  // case-insensitive
// sg.name === 'Singapore'
```

---

### `getJurisdictionCodes(): string[]`

Returns all available jurisdiction codes as an array of uppercase strings.

```typescript
import { getJurisdictionCodes } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const codes = getJurisdictionCodes()
// → ['GB', 'US', 'FR', 'DE', 'ES', 'IT', 'NL', 'CA', 'AU', 'NZ', ...]
// 28 entries total
```

---

### `getProfessionalBodies(code: string, profession?: ProfessionType): ProfessionalBody[]`

Returns professional bodies for a jurisdiction, optionally filtered by profession type. Returns `[]` for unknown codes.

```typescript
import { getProfessionalBodies } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

// All bodies in the UK
const allBodies = getProfessionalBodies('GB')
// → 21 professional bodies

// UK legal bodies only
const lawyers = getProfessionalBodies('GB', 'legal')
// → [
//     { id: 'law-society-ew', name: 'The Law Society of England and Wales',
//       profession: 'legal', hasPublicRegister: true,
//       registerUrl: 'https://www.lawsociety.org.uk/find-a-solicitor',
//       issuesDigitalCredentials: false, website: '...' },
//     { id: 'law-society-scotland', ... },
//     { id: 'law-society-ni', ... },
//     { id: 'bar-standards-board', ... },
//   ]

// Australian accounting bodies
const accountants = getProfessionalBodies('AU', 'accounting')
// → [{ name: 'Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand', ... }, ...]
```

---

### `getMutualRecognitionPartners(code: string): Jurisdiction[]`

Returns full Jurisdiction objects for all jurisdictions with formal mutual recognition agreements with the given jurisdiction.

```typescript
import { getMutualRecognitionPartners } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const partners = getMutualRecognitionPartners('AU')
// → [Jurisdiction(NZ), Jurisdiction(GB), Jurisdiction(CA), Jurisdiction(SG), Jurisdiction(HK)]

const frPartners = getMutualRecognitionPartners('FR')
// → EU/EEA neighbours plus adequacy-decision jurisdictions
```

---

### `isProfessionRegulated(code: string, profession: ProfessionType): boolean`

Checks whether a profession type has any registered bodies in the jurisdiction.

```typescript
import { isProfessionRegulated } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

isProfessionRegulated('GB', 'legal')       // true
isProfessionRegulated('GB', 'notary')      // true
isProfessionRegulated('US', 'medical')     // true
isProfessionRegulated('AE', 'social-work') // false (no registered body)
```

---

### `findJurisdictionsForProfession(profession: ProfessionType): Jurisdiction[]`

Returns all jurisdictions where a given profession is regulated (has at least one professional body).

```typescript
import { findJurisdictionsForProfession } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const medical = findJurisdictionsForProfession('medical')
// → Array of ~26 Jurisdiction objects

const architecture = findJurisdictionsForProfession('architecture')
// → Fewer jurisdictions — not all 28 regulate architecture

const veterinary = findJurisdictionsForProfession('veterinary')
// → Jurisdictions with registered veterinary bodies
```

---

### `getDigitalConsentAge(code: string): number`

Returns the minimum age for digital services without parental consent. Returns `16` for unknown jurisdictions (the GDPR default).

```typescript
import { getDigitalConsentAge } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

getDigitalConsentAge('GB')  // 13
getDigitalConsentAge('DE')  // 16
getDigitalConsentAge('US')  // 13 (COPPA)
getDigitalConsentAge('IN')  // 18
getDigitalConsentAge('BR')  // 12
getDigitalConsentAge('KR')  // 14
getDigitalConsentAge('XX')  // 16 (unknown — defaults to GDPR baseline)
```

---

### `getAgeOfMajority(code: string): number`

Returns the legal age of majority. Returns `18` for unknown jurisdictions.

```typescript
import { getAgeOfMajority } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

getAgeOfMajority('GB')  // 18
getAgeOfMajority('KR')  // 19
getAgeOfMajority('SG')  // 21
getAgeOfMajority('JP')  // 18 (lowered from 20 in 2022)
getAgeOfMajority('XX')  // 18 (safe default)
```

---

### `canTransferData(from: string, to: string): { allowed: boolean; mechanism?: string; notes?: string }`

Determines the legal mechanism for a cross-border personal data transfer. Returns `{ allowed: false }` only for unknown jurisdictions — all known transfers have a mechanism, though some require additional safeguards.

```typescript
import { canTransferData } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

canTransferData('FR', 'DE')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'eu-internal' }

canTransferData('FR', 'GB')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'adequacy-decision' }

canTransferData('GB', 'AU')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'mutual-recognition' }

canTransferData('US', 'BR')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'no-restrictions' }

canTransferData('DE', 'IN')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'safeguards-required',
//     notes: 'Requires Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or equivalent safeguards.' }

canTransferData('GB', 'GB')
// → { allowed: true, mechanism: 'domestic' }

canTransferData('XX', 'GB')
// → { allowed: false }
```

Transfer mechanisms:

| Mechanism | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|
| `domestic` | Same jurisdiction — no transfer |
| `eu-internal` | Both parties are EU/EEA member states |
| `adequacy-decision` | Destination has an EU or UK adequacy decision |
| `mutual-recognition` | Formal bilateral recognition agreement |
| `no-restrictions` | Source jurisdiction has no outbound data transfer restrictions |
| `safeguards-required` | Transfer permitted but requires SCCs or equivalent safeguards |

---

### `getAllLanguages(): string[]`

Returns a sorted array of all unique ISO 639-1 language codes represented across jurisdictions.

```typescript
import { getAllLanguages } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

getAllLanguages()
// → ['af', 'ar', 'ca', 'de', 'en', 'es', 'eu', 'fr', 'ga', 'gl', 'he',
//    'hi', 'id', 'it', 'ja', 'ko', 'ms', 'nl', 'pt', 'tr', 'ur', 'zh', ...]
```

---

### `getJurisdictionsByLanguage(lang: string): Jurisdiction[]`

Returns jurisdictions that list the given language code. Case-insensitive.

```typescript
import { getJurisdictionsByLanguage } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const frenchSpeaking = getJurisdictionsByLanguage('fr')
// → [Jurisdiction(FR), Jurisdiction(CA), Jurisdiction(BE), ...]

const spanishSpeaking = getJurisdictionsByLanguage('ES')  // case-insensitive
// → [Jurisdiction(ES), Jurisdiction(MX), Jurisdiction(AR), ...]

const arabicSpeaking = getJurisdictionsByLanguage('ar')
// → [Jurisdiction(AE), Jurisdiction(SA), Jurisdiction(IL), ...]
```

---

### `computeJurisdictionConfidence(code: string): JurisdictionConfidence | undefined`

Returns a detailed confidence breakdown (0–100) for how well a jurisdiction supports credential verification. Returns `undefined` for unknown codes.

```typescript
import { computeJurisdictionConfidence } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const confidence = computeJurisdictionConfidence('GB')
// → {
//     code: 'GB',
//     score: 80,
//     breakdown: {
//       professionalBodyCoverage: 20,   // 0–20: 1 pt per body, capped at 20
//       publicRegisterAvailability: 20, // 0–20: proportion with public registers × 20
//       digitalCredentialIssuance: 0,   // 0–15: proportion issuing digital credentials × 15
//       dataProtectionMaturity: 15,     // 0–15: composite of data protection features
//       mutualRecognition: 5,           // 0–10: 1 pt per partner, capped at 10
//       eSignatureRecognition: 10,      // 0 or 10
//       legalSystemScore: 10,           // 0–10: common-law/civil-law = 10, others lower
//     }
//   }

computeJurisdictionConfidence('XX')  // undefined
```

---

### `getJurisdictionConfidence(code: string): number`

Returns just the numeric confidence score (0–100). Returns `0` for unknown jurisdictions.

```typescript
import { getJurisdictionConfidence } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

getJurisdictionConfidence('GB')  // 80
getJurisdictionConfidence('US')  // ~75
getJurisdictionConfidence('XX')  // 0
```

---

### `rankJurisdictionsByConfidence(): JurisdictionConfidence[]`

Returns all 28 jurisdictions ranked by confidence score, highest first.

```typescript
import { rankJurisdictionsByConfidence } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

const ranked = rankJurisdictionsByConfidence()
// ranked[0].code === 'GB', ranked[0].score === 80
// ranked[1].code === 'AU', ...
// ranked[27].code === '...' (lowest scoring jurisdiction)
```

---

### `JURISDICTIONS: Record<string, Jurisdiction>`

The full jurisdiction dataset. Frozen with `Object.freeze` — read-only at runtime. Direct property access when you want to iterate or process in bulk.

```typescript
import { JURISDICTIONS } from 'jurisdiction-kit'

Object.keys(JURISDICTIONS).length  // 28
JURISDICTIONS['GB'].name           // 'United Kingdom'

// Iterate all jurisdictions
for (const [code, jurisdiction] of Object.entries(JURISDICTIONS)) {
  console.log(code, jurisdiction.dataProtection.name)
}
```

---

## Types

### `Jurisdiction`

```typescript
interface Jurisdiction {
  code: string                     // ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. 'GB')
  name: string                     // English country name
  nameLocal: string                // Local language name
  legalSystem: LegalSystem         // 'common-law' | 'civil-law' | 'mixed' | 'religious' | 'customary'
  languages: string[]              // ISO 639-1 language codes
  currency: string                 // ISO 4217 currency code
  professionalBodies: ProfessionalBody[]
  dataProtection: DataProtectionLaw
  childProtection: ChildProtectionLaw
  eSignatureRecognised: boolean    // e-signatures legally recognised
  mutualRecognition: string[]      // ISO codes of mutual recognition partners
  notes?: string
}
```

### `ProfessionalBody`

```typescript
interface ProfessionalBody {
  id: string                       // Short identifier (e.g. 'law-society-ew')
  name: string                     // Full name in original language
  nameEn?: string                  // English name (if different)
  profession: ProfessionType
  website: string
  hasPublicRegister: boolean       // Maintains a searchable public register
  registerUrl?: string             // URL of the public register
  issuesDigitalCredentials: boolean
  notes?: string
}
```

### `DataProtectionLaw`

```typescript
interface DataProtectionLaw {
  name: string                     // Short name (e.g. 'GDPR', 'UK GDPR + DPA 2018')
  fullName: string                 // Full legislative name
  year: number                     // Year enacted
  requiresExplicitConsent: boolean
  digitalConsentAge: number        // Minimum age for digital services without parental consent
  breachNotificationHours: number  // 0 = no statutory deadline
  rightToErasure: boolean
  rightToPortability: boolean
  crossBorderRestrictions: boolean
  maxRetentionDays: number         // 0 = no specific limit
  supervisoryAuthority: string
  notes?: string
}
```

### `ChildProtectionLaw`

```typescript
interface ChildProtectionLaw {
  name: string
  minAgeDigitalConsent: number
  ageOfMajority: number
  requiresParentalConsent: boolean
  enhancedProtections: boolean
  profilingRestrictions: boolean
}
```

### `JurisdictionConfidence`

```typescript
interface JurisdictionConfidence {
  code: string
  score: number   // 0–100 overall
  breakdown: {
    professionalBodyCoverage: number    // 0–20
    publicRegisterAvailability: number  // 0–20
    digitalCredentialIssuance: number   // 0–15
    dataProtectionMaturity: number      // 0–15
    mutualRecognition: number           // 0–10
    eSignatureRecognition: number       // 0 or 10
    legalSystemScore: number            // 0–10
  }
}
```

### Union types

```typescript
type LegalSystem = 'common-law' | 'civil-law' | 'mixed' | 'religious' | 'customary'

type ProfessionType =
  | 'legal'         // lawyers, solicitors, barristers
  | 'medical'       // doctors, dentists, nurses, opticians, osteopaths, chiropractors
  | 'notary'        // notaries public
  | 'accounting'    // chartered accountants, CPAs, auditors
  | 'engineering'   // chartered and professional engineers
  | 'teaching'      // teachers and educators
  | 'financial'     // financial advisers and actuaries
  | 'veterinary'    // veterinary surgeons
  | 'pharmacy'      // pharmacists
  | 'architecture'  // architects
  | 'social-work'   // social workers
```

---

## Use cases

- **Legal-tech compliance** — determine which professional bodies are authorised to practise in a target jurisdiction before launching a service
- **KYC / identity verification** — look up data protection law, digital consent age, and breach notification requirements for a user's jurisdiction
- **Cross-border data transfer analysis** — determine transfer mechanism (GDPR adequacy, SCCs, mutual recognition) before sharing personal data internationally
- **Age-gating** — retrieve `getDigitalConsentAge` per jurisdiction to set the correct minimum age for digital services
- **Professional credential verification** — find regulated bodies by country and profession, check whether a body maintains a searchable public register
- **Mutual recognition** — discover which jurisdictions have formal credential recognition agreements with a target jurisdiction
- **Jurisdiction ranking** — score and rank jurisdictions by how well they support professional credential verification

---

## Data methodology

- **Sources**: publicly available government registries, official regulatory authority websites, and legal databases
- **Currency**: data compiled in 2025 and reflects legislation as of that date — always verify with the relevant regulatory authority before production compliance decisions
- **Coverage**: 28 countries, weighted towards common-law and EU/EEA jurisdictions
- **Bundle size**: the full frozen dataset is always included (~3,400 lines of source). Not tree-shakeable — the data is the library.

---

- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/jurisdiction-kit)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/forgesworn/jurisdiction-kit)
